<!--
As part of the checklist below, you acknowledge you have reviewed the terms of service of a brand, to ensure we are granted permission to include this brand. Please link here to the terms you have reviewed, to make maintainer review easier. Ideally link to a section and/or paragraph.
-->

### Checklist

- [ ] I have reviewed the [forbidden brands](https://github.com/simple-icons/simple-icons/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md#forbidden-brands) list and confirm the brand I am submitting a PR for is not one of them, nor is it a subsidiary of one of those brands
- [ ] I have reviewed the brand's terms of service, and am confident we can add this icon
- [ ] I confirm that I did not use AI tools to create this PR, or that any AI assistance has been fully disclosed in this PR
- [ ] I updated the JSON data in `data/simple-icons.json`
- [ ] I optimized the icon with SVGO or SVGOMG
- [ ] The SVG `viewbox` is `0 0 24 24`

### Description

<!--
Anything relevant, for example:
  - Why did you pick the hex value?
  - Did you manually vectorize the logo?
  - Have you used multiple sources?
  - Did you use AI tools?
  - etc.
-->

prs.title: docs: remove outdated mongoose 5.x reference and update stale driver …
prs.body:
The Multi-mongos support section referenced `mongoose 5.x` in the current
v9 docs, which is misleading for users on newer versions.

The linked MongoDB driver URL also pointed to the old v3 API docs (now outdated).

Updated the sentence to remove the version reference and link to the
current MongoDB Node.js driver documentation.

Changes are limited to documentation only—no functional impact.

prs.title: docs: remove outdated 'alpha' label from Deno support in README
prs.body:
Deno support was introduced in Mongoose 6.8.0 and is no longer in alpha.

Removed the `(alpha)` label from the opening line and updated the
installation note to reflect current stable support.

Changes are limited to documentation only—no functional impact.

prs.title: docs: fix docs wording, typos, and stale link
docs: upgrade http:// links to https:// in connections.md
prs.body:
A few links in [docs/connections.md](cci:7://file:///Users/sandeshlendve/Desktop/GIT%20PR/git%20prs/mongoose/docs/connections.md:0:0-0:0) were still using `http://`.

This PR updates them to `https://` for consistency.

Changes are limited to documentation only—no functional impact.

prs.title: fix: prevent GFM table tokens from greedily capturing trailing newlines
prs.body:
### Summary
Following the fix for headings (#3513), I discovered that GFM table tokens also greedily capture multiple trailing newlines in their `raw` property. This PR brings the [table](cci:1://file:///Users/pushkargupta/Desktop/PR/marked/src/Tokenizer.ts:510:2-571:3) tokenizer into consistency with other block-level elements.

### Changes
- Updated [src/Tokenizer.ts](cci:7://file:///Users/pushkargupta/Desktop/PR/marked/src/Tokenizer.ts:0:0-0:0): Applied [rtrim(cap[0], '\n')](cci:1://file:///Users/pushkargupta/Desktop/PR/marked/src/helpers.ts:79:0-109:1) to the `raw` field in the [table()](cci:1://file:///Users/pushkargupta/Desktop/PR/marked/src/Tokenizer.ts:510:2-571:3) method.
- Updated [test/unit/marked.test.js](cci:7://file:///Users/pushkargupta/Desktop/PR/marked/test/unit/marked.test.js:0:0-0:0): Adjusted the [walkTokens](cci:1://file:///Users/pushkargupta/Desktop/PR/marked/test/unit/marked.test.js:848:8-851:9) assertion for tables to account for the newly separated [space](cci:1://file:///Users/pushkargupta/Desktop/PR/marked/src/Tokenizer.ts:70:2-78:3) tokens.

### Verification
- **Spec Compliance:** All 1735 spec tests (CommonMark & GFM) passed.
- **Unit Tests:** All existing unit tests passed after the structural update.
- **Consistency:** Verified that table tokens no longer include multiple trailing blank lines, matching the behavior of `hr` and `paragraph`.

Fixes #3513 (Consistency follow-up)

prs.title: refactor: remove deprecated fs.exists and modernize load.js syntax
prs.body:
## Description
This PR refactors `lib/config/load.js` to remove deprecated API usage and dead code fallbacks, improving overall code quality and maintainability.

## Changes
- **API Cleanup**: Removed unused `fs.exists` (deprecated) and dead `path.exists` fallbacks (obsolete since Node 0.8).
- **Modernization**:
    - Replaced the local `existsSync` variable with direct calls to `fs.existsSync`.
    - Converted all `var` declarations to `const` and `let` for better scoping.
- **Lint Fix**: Fixed a redundant `toString('utf8')` call on a variable that was already a string.

## Verification
- Successfully ran `npm run lint`.
- Verified that all 17 tests in `test/config/load.test.js` pass.

prs.title: refactor: modernize log utilities with ES6 classes and syntax
prs.body:
## Description
This PR modernizes the [lib/utils/log.js](cci:7://file:///Users/pushkargupta/Desktop/PR/nodemon/lib/utils/log.js:0:0-0:0) module by refactoring it to use ES6 classes and modern JavaScript syntax (ES6+). These changes improve code readability and maintainability while ensuring full backward compatibility with the existing codebase.

## Changes
- **ES6 Class Refactor**: Converted the legacy [Logger](cci:2://file:///Users/pushkargupta/Desktop/PR/nodemon/lib/utils/log.js:40:0-73:1) constructor and prototype-based methods into a modern `class Logger` structure.
- **Modern Syntax Adoption**:
    - Replaced `var` with [const](cci:1://file:///Users/pushkargupta/Desktop/PR/nodemon/lib/utils/log.js:41:2-43:3) and `let` for improved scoping.
    - Adopted template literals for cleaner and more readable string construction.
    - Used arrow functions for more concise callbacks.
    - Used shorthand property names in object literals.
- **Code Cleanup**: Removed legacy architectural "question" comments to streamline the source code.
- **Backward Compatibility**: Maintained the factory function export pattern. This ensures that internal consumers calling the module as a function (e.g., [require('./log')(val)](cci:1://file:///Users/pushkargupta/Desktop/PR/nodemon/lib/utils/log.js:45:2-47:3)) continue to function without modification.

## Verification
- **Linting**: Passed `npm run lint` with no issues.
- **Automated Tests**: Verified that [test/config/load-logging.test.js](cci:7://file:///Users/pushkargupta/Desktop/PR/nodemon/test/config/load-logging.test.js:0:0-0:0) passes, confirming that logging configuration remains intact.
- **Manual Testing**: Verified both the factory function and the class constructor directly to ensure output formatting, color support, and debug toggling work as expected.

prs.body:
## Description

This PR fixes the broken `source` URL for the **Vitess** icon, which was previously pointing to an obsolete and defunct Netlify domain (`cncf-branding.netlify.app`). It has been updated to point directly to the officially maintained [Vitess website](https://vitess.io).

This addresses one of the broken URLs documented in #11901.

- **Old URL**: `https://cncf-branding.netlify.app/projects/vitess/` (Broken)
- **New URL**: `https://vitess.io` (Verified Live)

## Checklist

- [x] I have read the [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/simple-icons/simple-icons/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md) guidelines.
- [x] I checked that the updated URL is live and points to the official maintainers.
- [x] I ran the required linter commands (`npm run lint`) and all checks passed perfectly without trailing format errors.
- [x] The `source` URL does not contain any tracking identifiers.

prs.title: docs: fix redundant text and upgrade http link in installation section
prs.body:
## What does this PR do?

Cleans up the Installation section in `README.md`:

- Removes duplicate "Then" — the original text had "First install ... Then: Then install..." which was a clear copy-paste redundancy.
- Merges the two sentences into one concise instruction.
- Upgrades the Node.js link from `http://nodejs.org/` to `https://nodejs.org/` for security best practices.

## Changes
- `README.md` — Installation section, line ~42

prs.title: feat: add :focus-visible support for better keyboard accessibility
prs.body:
## Description
Adds `:focus-visible` CSS support for better keyboard accessibility.

## Changes
- Shows 2px outline for keyboard navigation (Tab)
- Hides outline for mouse clicks
- Uses `:where()` for low specificity
- Covers buttons, inputs, links, and other interactive elements

## Testing
✅ Built and tested in browser - keyboard shows outlines, mouse clicks don't

## Impact
- Improves keyboard accessibility
- Better WCAG 2.1 compliance
- No breaking changes
- 96%+ browser support

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Style**
  * Improved focus styling for interactive elements to boost keyboard navigation visibility: added a clear, accessible focus outline for keyboard users while suppressing the outline for non-keyboard (mouse) interactions to reduce visual clutter.

<sub>✏️ Tip: You can customize this high-level summary in your review settings.</sub>
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: feat: add transformer-remove-empty-lines
prs.body:
### Description

This PR adds a new transformer `transformerRemoveEmptyLines` that removes empty lines from the generated HTML. This is useful for creating compact code displays.

### Linked Issues

Fixes #1206

### Additional context

I have added a test case in `packages/transformers/test/remove-empty-lines.test.ts` and verified it passes.

issues.title: feat: add transformer-remove-empty-lines
issues.body:
### Clear and concise description of the problem

I want to remove empty lines from the code output so that the display is more compact.
I intend to submit a PR for this issue.

### Suggested solution

In `packages/transformers`, we could provide a `transformerRemoveEmptyLines` that filters out lines containing only whitespace.

### Alternative

None considered.

### Additional context

None.

### Validations

- [x] Follow our [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/shikijs/shiki/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
- [x] Read the [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/shikijs/shiki/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
- [x] Check that there isn't already an issue that request the same feature to avoid creating a duplicate.
